title: On the Value of Daily Labor
author: From: The Journal of Henry David Thoreau
content: He who works with his hands is a laborer; he who works with his hands and his head is a craftsman; he who works with his hands, his head, and his heart is an artist.
action: Action: Bring more intentionality to a routine task today to elevate it from 'labor' to 'craft'.

title: The OODA Loop
author: From: John Boyd
content: Observe, Orient, Decide, Act. The speed of this cycle is your greatest competitive advantage in a fast-changing market.
action: Action: Identify a bottleneck in your current decision-making cycle and remove one step.

title: The Minimum Viable Product
author: From: Eric Ries
content: Build the simplest version that solves the primary problem. Perfection is an iteration, not a starting point.
action: Action: Strip your next deliverable down to the single most critical feature.

title: The Endowment Effect
author: From: Dan Ariely
content: We overvalue things simply because we created them. Objectivity requires looking at your work through a neutral lens.
action: Action: Critique a project you've completed as if you were an outside consultant hired to improve it.

title: The Power of Inaction
author: From: Rolf Dobelli
content: In a complex system, 'doing something' is often just a reaction to anxiety. Strategic patience is a rare and profitable skill.
action: Action: Identify one decision you are rushing and force yourself to wait 24 hours.
